title: Fresh Syrian Buckwheat Dumplings
description: A traditional Syrian dish made with buckwheat flour, these dumplings are soft, chewy, and perfect for kids. They can be served as a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together buckwheat flour, all-purpose flour, and salt.
Gradually add warm water, stirring with a fork until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the dumplings to the skillet, cooking for 2-3 minutes on each side, until lightly browned.
Serve the dumplings with chopped parsley, lemon juice, and a side of your favorite dipping sauce.
